title: Source code configuration
text: Source code configuration The PDF recommends keeping headers and source files managed independently: - Header files under the module include directory - Source files under the module source directory Representative source layout: ```cpp /******************************************************************************* * Copyright 1999-2012, ALTIBASE Corporation or its subsidiaries. * All rights reserved. *************************************...
summary: Source code configuration

title: Use only `/* ... */` comments
text: Use only `/* ... */` comments - Only `/* ... */` style comments are allowed. - Every file should have a file header comment.
summary: Use only `/* ... */` comments

title: Comment every file
text: Comment every file - Only `/* ... */` style comments are allowed. - Every file should have a file header comment.
summary: Comment every file

title: Comment every function
text: Comment every function - Only `/* ... */` style comments are allowed. - Every file should have a file header comment.
summary: Comment every function

title: Do not leave sections of code commented out
text: Do not leave sections of code commented out - Only `/* ... */` style comments are allowed. - Every file should have a file header comment.
summary: Do not leave sections of code commented out

title: Do not use bit-field data types
text: Do not use bit-field data types Key reminders from the PDF: - Do not use bit-fields. - When using `typedef struct`, repeat the same type name at the end. - Use `void` explicitly for functions that take no arguments.
summary: Do not use bit-field data types

title: Repeat the struct type name in `typedef struct`
text: Repeat the struct type name in `typedef struct` Key reminders from the PDF: - Do not use bit-fields. - When using `typedef struct`, repeat the same type name at the end. - Use `void` explicitly for functions that take no arguments.

title: Use `#if defined()` and `#if !defined()` instead of `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`
text: Use `#if defined()` and `#if !defined()` instead of `#ifdef` and `#ifndef` - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Use `#if defined()` and `#if !defined()` instead of `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`

title: Do not use double quotes in include definitions
text: Do not use double quotes in include definitions - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Do not use double quotes in include definitions

title: All `#define` constants must be uppercase
text: All `#define` constants must be uppercase - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: All `#define` constants must be uppercase

title: Do not include system library header files directly
text: Do not include system library header files directly - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Do not include system library header files directly

title: Use `#ifndef` and `#endif` include guards
text: Use `#ifndef` and `#endif` include guards - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Use `#ifndef` and `#endif` include guards

title: Preprocessor `#` starts in the first column
text: Preprocessor `#` starts in the first column - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Preprocessor `#` starts in the first column

title: Parenthesize every parameter occurrence in function-like macros
text: Parenthesize every parameter occurrence in function-like macros - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.

text: Avoid side effects in arguments to unsafe macros - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.

text: Keep macros within 10 lines - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.

title: Control flow introduction
text: Control flow introduction - Do not use `continue`. - Always provide a `default` branch in every `switch`. - Keep nested block depth at `<= 5`.

title: Keep cyclomatic complexity within 10
text: Keep cyclomatic complexity within 10 - Do not use `continue`. - Always provide a `default` branch in every `switch`. - Keep nested block depth at `<= 5`.
summary: Keep cyclomatic complexity within 10

title: Do not place `TEST` macros under `EXCEPTION_END`
text: Do not place `TEST` macros under `EXCEPTION_END` The PDF has a separate section for Altibase error handling, beyond the `ALTI-ERR-*` rules. Core guidance: - Functions that can fail return `IDE_RC`. - Functions that cannot fail may return their direct value instead.

title: The third parameter to `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove` must not depend on the second buffer
text: The third parameter to `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove` must not depend on the second buffer - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: The third parameter to `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove` must not depend on the second buffer

title: Do not perform zero-length allocations
text: Do not perform zero-length allocations - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Do not perform zero-length allocations

title: Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` as the allocation size for `malloc`, `calloc`, or `realloc`
text: Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` as the allocation size for `malloc`, `calloc`, or `realloc` - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` as the allocation size for `malloc`, `calloc`, or `realloc`

title: Null-terminate character strings as required
text: Null-terminate character strings as required - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Null-terminate character strings as required

title: Use `'\0'` as the null termination character
text: Use `'\0'` as the null termination character - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Use `'\0'` as the null termination character

title: Allocate and free memory in the same module and at the same abstraction level
text: Allocate and free memory in the same module and at the same abstraction level - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Allocate and free memory in the same module and at the same abstraction level

title: Assign `NULL` immediately after `free()`
text: Assign `NULL` immediately after `free()` - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Assign `NULL` immediately after `free()`

title: Do not use unbounded functions
text: Do not use unbounded functions - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Do not use unbounded functions

title: Never return a reference to a local object
text: Never return a reference to a local object - In `memcpy`, `strncpy`, and `memmove`, the third argument should not depend on the source buffer size. - Do not perform zero-length allocations. - Do not use `sizeof(pointer)` to compute allocation size for the pointed-to object.
summary: Never return a reference to a local object

title: Do not call system library functions directly
text: Do not call system library functions directly - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Do not call system library functions directly

title: Do not use directory separators directly
text: Do not use directory separators directly - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.
summary: Do not use directory separators directly

title: Use `IDL_INVALID_HANDLE` for file and socket handle error checks
text: Use `IDL_INVALID_HANDLE` for file and socket handle error checks - Prefer `#if defined(MY_MACRO)` and `#if !defined(MY_MACRO)` over `#ifdef` and `#ifndef`. - Always use angle brackets for includes: ```cpp #include <qdtest.h> ``` - Macro names must be uppercase. - Do not include system headers directly.

title: Do not use primitive format specifiers directly

summary: Do not use primitive format specifiers directly
